informal_statement.text:
Given a non-empty slice of integers, return the maximum sum over all contiguous non-empty subslices.

Benchmark note (arrays_max_subarray_005): Variant 5 stresses monotonicity or stability properties implied by the reference implementation.
Authoring lens: Lens 5 highlights obligations that must remain checkable against the behavioral oracle.
required_properties: the returned value equals the maximum sum over all contiguous non-empty subslices
there exist indices i <= j with i < arr.len() and j <= arr.len() such that arr[i..j] sums to the returned value
no contiguous non-empty subslice sums to a strictly greater value
edge_cases: all negative elements (max is the largest single element)
all positive elements (max is the whole slice)
a single large negative element interleaved among positives
